backend/main.mo: Main actor for the Beast Clipping ICP backend, defining all data types (UserProfile, VideoClip, FeedbackSubmission, etc.) and including authorization, storage, and HTTP outcall mixins with migration support on upgrade.
backend/migration.mo: Migration module that defines old and new actor state shapes and a `run` function that passes all state fields through unchanged during canister upgrades.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report mapping feature IDs and titles to the frontend/backend files and components that implement them, along with test IDs and confidence levels.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: Auto-generated text file containing one-sentence summaries of each frontend source file, describing the purpose and key behaviors of components, hooks, and pages.
frontend/.env: Frontend environment file containing the Vite-exposed Google OAuth client ID for the Beast Clipping app.
frontend/.env.example: Template environment file with a detailed step-by-step guide for obtaining and configuring a Google OAuth client ID, including troubleshooting tips.
frontend/.gitignore: Standard gitignore for a Vite/Node frontend project, excluding node_modules, dist output, editor files, and environment variable files.
frontend/index.css: Root CSS file that sets up Tailwind directives and defines OKLCH-based CSS custom properties for light and dark color themes including sidebar and chart tokens.
frontend/index.html: HTML entry point for the Beast Clipping frontend, mounting the React app at `#root` and loading the main TypeScript module.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Sets up the React app with TanStack Router, React Query, and theme providers, defining all top-level routes and wrapping them in account/system status guards.
frontend/src/components/AccountStatusErr: Displays a full-screen error UI when a user's account is banned or suspended, with a logout button.
frontend/src/components/AccountStatusGua: A guard component that wraps children and is intended to block access based on account status; currently passes through unconditionally.
frontend/src/components/ActivityLogTable: Renders a searchable table of activity logs fetched via useActivityLogs, showing user ID, action, and relative timestamp.
Displays admin authentication debug information including current principal, admin list, and initialization status, with copy and refresh utilities.
frontend/src/components/AdminErrorBounda: A React class-based error boundary that catches errors in the admin panel and renders a detailed error card with retry and home navigation options.
frontend/src/components/AdminList.tsx: Fetches and displays the list of admin principals using useAdminList, showing each admin's short ID and full principal with loading and error states.
Provides a form to add a new admin by principal ID using useAddAdmin, and renders the AdminList component below it.
frontend/src/components/AdminMessaging.t: A form component that allows admins to send messages to specific users by entering a recipient user ID and message body, using the useSendAdminMessage hook.
frontend/src/components/AdminPanel.tsx: The main admin panel component that renders collapsible sections for system controls, analytics, user management, messaging, feedback, and activity logs, visible only to owners.
frontend/src/components/AppAnalytics.tsx: Displays admin analytics including total clips count, trending count, and a bar chart of upload activity over the last 24 hours using Recharts.
frontend/src/components/AppShell.tsx: Layout shell component that renders a side navigation for desktop, a main content area, and a fixed bottom navigation bar for mobile.
frontend/src/components/BottomNavigation: Mobile bottom navigation bar that renders nav items (Clips, Trending, Feed, Gallery, Profile, Messages) filtered by authentication state, with a feedback modal.
frontend/src/components/BurnInCaptionsTo: A toggle button component that activates or deactivates burn-in captions, with active/inactive visual states.
frontend/src/components/CaptionEditor.ts: An AI caption editor with a textarea (500 char limit), emoji quick-insert buttons, copy-to-clipboard, and markdown formatting hints via a tooltip.
frontend/src/components/ChannelConnectio: Displays the YouTube channel connection status (loading, connected, or disconnected) using the useYouTubeChannel hook.
frontend/src/components/ClipCard.tsx: A card component displaying a clip's thumbnail, viral score, duration, and action buttons for downloading, sharing, Discord, YouTube, and minting to gallery.
frontend/src/components/ClipList.tsx: Renders a responsive grid of ClipCard components from the useClips hook, with loading skeletons and an empty state message.
Renders AI-generated clip suggestions and trending clips as clickable buttons with viral score badges, allowing users to select a suggestion to populate timestamp controls.
frontend/src/components/ClipTimestampCon: Form component for creating a named video clip by specifying start/end timestamps, with support for pre-filling from suggestions and saving via the clip creation mutation.
frontend/src/components/DetectHypeButton: Button that simulates hype peak detection across a video's duration, returning a sorted array of timestamp peaks after a fake async delay.
frontend/src/components/ExportFormatDrop: Dropdown component for selecting a video export format (MP4, GIF, or MP3) with icons, rendered as a floating menu above the trigger button.
frontend/src/components/FeedbackModal.ts: Modal dialog for submitting user feedback as either a Feature Request or Bug Report, with title and description fields backed by the useFeedbackSubmit mutation.
frontend/src/components/FeedbackSubmissi: Displays a list of all feedback submissions with type icons, submitter ID, relative timestamps, and a delete button per entry.
frontend/src/components/GreenScreenOverl: Renders a 2x2 grid of toggle buttons for applying colored visual overlays (green, red, blue, gold), tracking the active overlay via props.
frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx: Passthrough layout wrapper retained for compatibility; actual layout is handled by AppShell.
frontend/src/components/MemeOverlayPanel: Collapsible panel combining SoundEffectButtons and GreenScreenOverlayButtons under a single 'MEME OVERLAYS' accordion UI.
frontend/src/components/MintToGalleryBut: Button that mints a clip to the gallery using the useMintClip hook, showing a loading spinner during minting and a 'MINTED' badge if already minted.
frontend/src/components/NotificationBell: Renders a bell icon button with an unread count badge that toggles a dropdown panel listing notifications with mark-read and mark-all-read actions.
frontend/src/components/NotificationToas: Displays transient toast popups for new unread notifications, auto-dismissing each after 5 seconds and tracking already-shown IDs to avoid duplicates.
Provides quick preset buttons to set clip start/end times to the last 30s, last 60s, or an auto-detected highlight segment of a video.
frontend/src/components/PausedScreen.tsx: Full-screen maintenance page shown when the app is paused, with a button to re-check system status via React Query cache invalidation.
frontend/src/components/ProfileSetup.tsx: Modal form that collects a display name and saves a new user profile with a default role on first login.
frontend/src/components/SendToDiscordBut: Button that opens a modal for entering a Discord webhook URL and POSTs a formatted embed with clip details to that webhook.
Dropdown share menu that lets users copy a YouTube timestamped link, share to X (Twitter), or copy a Discord-formatted message for a clip.
frontend/src/components/SideNavigation.t: Persistent sidebar with logo, authenticated user info, role-filtered navigation links, a notification bell, feedback modal trigger, and login/logout handling via Internet Identity.
frontend/src/components/SlowMoToggle.tsx: A toggle button component that activates/deactivates a slow-motion mode, styled with cyan neon when active.
Displays a scrollable list of AI-detected smart clip segments from a transcript, allowing users to click and seek to each moment.
frontend/src/components/SoundEffectButto: Renders a grid of buttons for playing preset sound effects (airhorn, bruh, mission failed, victory) using the useSoundEffect hook.
frontend/src/components/SystemControls.t: Provides system control buttons (pause/resume, restart, shutdown) with confirmation dialogs, managing global system status via useSystemStatus.
frontend/src/components/TikTokifyPresetB: A toggle button that activates a TikTokify preset, styled with pink accents when active.
frontend/src/components/TimelineScrubber: An interactive video timeline scrubber with a seek bar, playhead, hype markers, and a hover tooltip showing timestamps and a YouTube thumbnail preview.
Displays a scrollable list of transcript segments with timestamps, highlights the currently active segment, and allows clicking to seek to a segment.
Shows a ranked list of up to 5 trending clips with their trending scores, fetched via useAdminStats.
frontend/src/components/UserIdDisplay.ts: Displays the authenticated user's short user ID and full principal ID with copy-to-clipboard buttons for each.
frontend/src/components/UserMessages.tsx: Displays a user's messages list and allows viewing individual messages with a reply form, using hooks for fetching messages and sending admin messages.
frontend/src/components/UserRoleBadge.ts: Renders a styled badge component for a user's role (owner, admin, friend, or default user) with corresponding icons and colors.
frontend/src/components/UserStatusManage: Admin UI component for listing, searching, and managing user roles and statuses, with copy-to-clipboard support for principal IDs.
frontend/src/components/VerticalClipPrev: Renders a 9:16 aspect ratio preview card for a video clip, showing its thumbnail or a fallback title placeholder.
frontend/src/components/VerticalModeTogg: A toggle button component that switches vertical mode on/off, styled with active/inactive states using a smartphone icon.
frontend/src/components/VideoUrlForm.tsx: Form component for inputting a YouTube URL to load a video and optionally trigger AI-based clip suggestion generation.
Displays a color-coded badge showing a viral score with different styles for high (≥80), medium (≥50), and low scores.
frontend/src/components/YouTubePlayer.ts: Renders a responsive 16:9 YouTube iframe embed player given a video ID.
frontend/src/hooks/useActivityLogs.ts: Provides an in-memory activity log store with a helper to add logs and a React Query hook to fetch them.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: Creates and caches an authenticated or anonymous backend actor using React Query, reinitializing and invalidating dependent queries when the identity changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useAddAdmin.ts: Stub mutation hook for adding an admin by user ID; logs the action locally and invalidates the admin list query on success.
frontend/src/hooks/useAdminDebug.ts: Fetches admin debug information (principal, admin status) from the backend actor, with placeholders for unimplemented backend methods.
frontend/src/hooks/useAdminList.ts: Query hook that returns the list of admin principals; currently returns an empty array as the backend method is unavailable.
frontend/src/hooks/useAdminStats.ts: Fetches admin statistics including total clips and trending analytics from the local clips store, with helper hooks for individual stat values.
Mutation hook that simulates AI-based clip suggestion generation for a YouTube video ID, returning mock suggestions after a delay.
frontend/src/hooks/useClipCreation.ts: Provides a mutation hook for creating video clips locally, calculating a viral score based on duration and title keywords, storing the clip in an in-memory store, and invalidating related queries.
frontend/src/hooks/useClips.ts: Maintains an in-memory clip store with add/remove helpers and exposes a query hook to fetch clips and a mutation hook to delete them, invalidating the clips query on changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useClipTimestamps.ts: Manages start/end timestamp state (minutes and seconds) for clip creation, with helpers for validation, formatting the time range, and converting to/from total seconds.
frontend/src/hooks/useContentEntries.ts: Manages an in-memory content entry store with hooks for querying, creating, updating, and deleting entries, each showing toast notifications on success or failure.
frontend/src/hooks/useDeleteFeedback.ts: Provides a mutation hook to delete feedback by ID, invalidating the feedbackSubmissions query and showing a toast notification on completion.
frontend/src/hooks/useDownloadClip.ts: Provides a mutation hook that simulates a clip download by showing a preparation toast and resolving after a short delay, then notifying success or failure.
frontend/src/hooks/useFeedbackSubmission: Exposes a query hook that fetches all feedback submissions from the in-memory feedback store, enabled only when an actor is available.
frontend/src/hooks/useFeedbackSubmit.ts: Maintains an in-memory feedback store and provides a mutation hook to submit new feedback entries, invalidating the feedbackSubmissions query on success.
frontend/src/hooks/useGetOwnRole.ts: Queries the backend actor for the caller's user role, returning null if unavailable or on error, with a 30-second stale time.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Implements an Internet Identity authentication context and provider using DFINITY's AuthClient, exposing identity state, login/clear functions, and status flags to child components.
frontend/src/hooks/useIsOwner.ts: Queries the backend actor to check if the caller is an admin, returning a boolean with a 30-second stale time.
frontend/src/hooks/useMintClip.ts: Provides a mintClip function that simulates an NFT minting delay, adds the clip to the minted collectibles store, and triggers a notification and toast on success.
frontend/src/hooks/useMintedCollectibles: Maintains an in-memory store of minted collectibles with listener-based reactivity, exposing a hook that returns the collectibles list only when the user is authenticated.
frontend/src/hooks/useMyMessages.ts: Queries an in-memory messages store filtered by the given user ID (as sender or recipient), enabled only when an actor and user ID are present.
frontend/src/hooks/useNotifications.ts: Manages a global in-memory notification list with listener-based reactivity, exposing hooks to read notifications, count unread items, and mark them as read.
frontend/src/hooks/usePostToYouTube.ts: Provides a stub mutation hook for posting clips to YouTube that always returns a not-available response with an informational toast.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Provides hooks for fetching and saving the caller's user profile from an in-memory store (supplemented by the backend role call) and a stub for storing Google OAuth credentials.
frontend/src/hooks/useScheduledUploads.t: Provides React Query hooks for managing scheduled uploads using an in-memory store, supporting listing, adding, and deleting scheduled upload entries.
frontend/src/hooks/useSendAdminMessage.t: Mutation hook that creates and stores an AdminMessage locally in the message store, then invalidates the myMessages query cache.
frontend/src/hooks/useSetUserRole.ts: Mutation hook that calls the backend actor's assignCallerUserRole method to update a user's role and invalidates the userRoles query on success.
frontend/src/hooks/useSetUserStatus.ts: Stub mutation hook for updating a user's status; logs the action locally since the backend doesn't implement this method.
frontend/src/hooks/useSocialFeed.ts: Query hook that builds a social feed from the local clips store, mapping clips to FeedItem objects sorted by timestamp with a 30-second refetch interval.
frontend/src/hooks/useSoundEffect.ts: Hook that provides a playSound function generating synthesized audio tones (airhorn, bruh, mission-failed, victory) using the Web Audio API.
frontend/src/hooks/useSystemStatus.ts: Manages a global system status value using a simple listener pattern, exposing a hook to read the current status and a setter to update it.
frontend/src/hooks/useTranscriptGenerato: Generates mock transcript segments from sample gaming phrases for a given video duration, flagging segments containing gaming keywords as smart clips.
frontend/src/hooks/useTrendingClips.ts: Query hook that returns the top 10 clips from the local clips store sorted by score descending.
frontend/src/hooks/useUserRoles.ts: Query hook that is intended to fetch all user roles but returns an empty array since the backend doesn't implement getAllUserRoles.
frontend/src/hooks/useYouTubeChannel.ts: Query hook that stubs YouTube channel connection data, always returning a disconnected state since the backend lacks YouTube channel methods.
Utility hook exposing functions to validate YouTube URLs via regex and extract the 11-character video ID from a URL.
frontend/src/index.css: Global stylesheet defining Tailwind base/components/utilities, CSS custom properties for a dark cyberpunk theme, and reusable component classes like glass-card, neon-glow, and cyberpunk-btn.
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that mounts the React app with QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider wrapping the root App component.
frontend/src/pages/ContentManager.tsx: Admin-only page for creating, editing, and deleting content entries, with login and permission guards and a dialog form for entry management.
frontend/src/pages/MessagesPage.tsx: Page that displays the UserMessages component for authenticated users, or a login prompt for unauthenticated visitors.
frontend/src/pages/MyClipsPage.tsx: Simple page that renders the ClipList component under a 'My Clips' heading for displaying the user's saved clips.
frontend/src/pages/MyGalleryPage.tsx: Displays a grid of the authenticated user's minted NFT collectibles on ICP, showing thumbnails, clip time ranges, and viral scores. Requires login via Internet Identity; shows empty state or loading skeletons as needed.
frontend/src/pages/OAuthCallback.tsx: Handles the OAuth redirect callback by extracting the authorization code from URL params and redirecting to home after a brief success/error display. No actual backend OAuth exchange is performed.
frontend/src/pages/ProfilePage.tsx: Shows the authenticated user's profile including their principal ID, display name, and stats (total clips, average score, viral clips count), plus a chronological clip activity list. Requires Internet Identity login.
frontend/src/pages/Scheduler.tsx: Provides a UI for scheduling video clips for YouTube upload, including a form to select a clip and datetime, a list of existing scheduled uploads with delete support, and YouTube channel connection status.
frontend/src/pages/SocialFeedPage.tsx: Renders a public activity feed showing recent clip creations from all users, with avatar initials, clip thumbnails, time ranges, and relative timestamps. Displays loading skeletons and an empty state when no activity exists.
frontend/src/pages/TrendingPage.tsx: Shows top trending clips ranked by viral/trending score, including a top-5 analytics leaderboard and a responsive grid of clip cards. Uses admin stats and trending clips hooks for data.
frontend/src/types/app.ts: Defines all app-specific TypeScript interfaces and types used across the frontend, including UserProfile, VideoClip, ScheduledUpload, AdminMessage, and various supporting types. These are local types independent of the backend Candid interface.
frontend/src/utils/urlParams.ts: Utility functions for reading, persisting, and clearing URL parameters from both query strings and hash-based routing, with sessionStorage fallback support.
frontend/src/utils/userIdGenerator.ts: Generates a deterministic 8-character base36 user ID from a principal string using a simple hash function.
frontend/src/utils/youtubeOAuthDebug.ts: Debugging utility class for tracking the YouTube OAuth flow, including logging initiation, callbacks, token validation, backend calls, and common error diagnosis.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration defining a cyberpunk dark theme with neon cyan/purple colors, custom fonts (Orbitron, Rajdhani), neon box shadows, and glassmorphism utilities.
project_state.json: Project state manifest describing a gaming-focused AI video clipping web app with cyberpunk UI, ICP blockchain integration, and a list of existing and planned features.
